partial_progress.details: Deep analysis of CanonicalIrreflexivity.lean reveals fundamental blocker: naming set approach requires deriving ¬p ∈ M' from H(¬p) ∈ M', but p ∈ M' (by construction) so ¬p ∉ M' (by consistency). Without T-axiom, no contradiction is derivable. The naming set {p, H(¬p)} ∪ atomFreeSubset extends to a valid MCS M' under strict semantics.

blocking_issues.issue: FUNDAMENTAL: naming set proof cannot derive contradiction - p ∈ M' and H(¬p) ∈ M' is consistent under strict semantics without T-axiom
blocking_issues.analysis: The proof tried to derive ¬p ∈ M' from H(¬p) ∈ M' using T-axiom H(φ)→φ. Under strict semantics, T-axiom is invalid. Seriality H(φ)→P(φ) gives P(¬p)=¬H(p) but NOT ¬p. Even with seriality, cannot derive ¬p ∈ M'. The MCS M' containing both p and H(¬p) is semantically valid: p true at current time, ¬p true at all strictly past times.

blocking_issues.issue: derive_F_to_FF needs derivation of Fφ → FFφ from GGφ → Gφ

recommendations: Option A: Prove irreflexivity only for discrete frames (seriality available)
Option B: Find completely different proof strategy not using naming set
Option C: Research whether strict-semantics irreflexivity is even provable with base axioms alone
Option D: Consider if the research-003 claim was based on incorrect analysis

review_reason: Phase 4 BLOCKED: fundamental issue with naming set proof under strict semantics. The claim in research-003 that irreflexivity becomes 'nearly definitional' appears incorrect - the proof requires T-axiom (invalid under strict semantics) or seriality (not a base axiom). Need user decision on how to proceed.
